baseline: no-skill (general investing knowledge + raw yfinance, no skill scripts/doctrine)

evals.name: eval-gate-fail-diagnosis
evals.expected_output: Runs the hard-gate check first and cites it; verdict AVOID because a hard gate fails (currently trend_template, score 6/8 live); names the failing leg; does NOT rescue the gate with strong fundamentals/narrative; provides no position sizing, stop orders, or R:R targets; cites live tool numbers, not remembered priors.
assertions: runs/cites the hard-gate (qualify) check before deepening
verdict is AVOID / do-not-buy
names the failing gate leg (trend template / not in a confirmed uptrend)
does NOT override the gate fail with fundamentals or story
no position sizing, no stop-loss orders, no R:R/expectancy targets
cites live numeric values from tools (price/MA/RS), not generic recall
no coined labels (Minervini, SEPA, VCP, Trend Template, Code 33, power play, pocket pivot) in user-facing text

evals.name: eval-discovery
evals.prompt: 지금 뭐 살 만한 거 없어?
evals.expected_output: Reads market regime first (leadership + breadth), NOT distribution-day counting; surfaces leaders rather than defaulting to familiar mega-caps; gates each candidate through the hard-gate check before endorsing; provides no position sizing.
assertions: assesses market regime/context before naming buys
regime read is leadership/breadth-based, not distribution-day counting
surfaces screened leaders, not just famous mega-cap defaults
candidates are passed through the hard-gate (qualify) check
no position sizing / allocation percentages
no coined labels in user-facing text

evals.name: eval-convergence-compare
evals.prompt: 마이크론(MU)이랑 애플(AAPL) 둘 중에 지금 사기에 뭐가 더 나아?
evals.expected_output: Both clear the hard gates (Stage 2 + trend template 8/8 live). Distinguishes them on the conditional funnel: MU has accelerating fundamentals (EPS/sales/margin all improving), AAPL is a mature mega-cap with no acceleration. Reasons by AND-convergence (a passed gate + a failed fundamental leg = not a trade, not an average); applies good-company-vs-good-stock and acceleration-over-level; does NOT collapse to a single 0-100 score; no position sizing.
assertions: recognizes both names clear the structural hard gates
differentiates on fundamentals: MU accelerating vs AAPL not
uses AND-convergence reasoning, not a weighted average that splits the difference
applies 'good company != good stock' / acceleration over level
does NOT anchor on a single 0-100 composite score
no position sizing
no coined labels in user-facing text
